title: PowerWords
description: A published game addon built in Lua that hooks into the game's native API to respond to player events and trigger configurable actions. Working within a live, third-party API environment gave me experience with event-driven programming and shipping software to real users via CurseForge.

title: Personal Portfolio
description: A responsive personal portfolio built to present my skills, experience, and projects in one place. Designed and developed from scratch, this site gave me hands-on experience with React and TypeScript while focusing on clean UI, smooth animations, and a polished user experience.

title: Texas A&M Esports Infrastructure
description: Built the full technical backbone for a university esports organization, including a Discord bot, a secure REST API backend, and a Postgres database, all hosted on a GCP Compute Engine instance. Integrated with third-party payment and script automation tools to streamline membership management. This project was my first large-scale system design effort, covering deployment, cloud infrastructure, and real-world reliability requirements.

title: College Notes
description: A mobile app built to solve a personal need for better note and schedule organization during college. Built natively in Kotlin using Jetpack Compose and the MVVM architecture pattern, this project deepened my understanding of Android development, clean architecture, and state management in mobile UIs.

title: Video Game Design Portfolio
description: A dedicated site built to showcase personal game development projects. Created to practice frontend skills in React and explore how to present creative work effectively on the web.

title: Olympus Navigator
description: A Discord bot that connects to a MySQL database to store and retrieve user data on demand. Built to automate community management tasks, this project taught me how to design a bot with persistent state and gave me my first experience integrating a backend database with a real-time messaging platform.

title: TAMU Personal Page
description: A personal website built as part of a university web development course, using only HTML, CSS, and vanilla JavaScript with no frameworks. Served as my introduction to frontend development and the foundations of building for the web.

title: The Golden Expansion
description: A content mod that extends an existing game with new items, plants, and a custom dimension. Built using the Forge Mod Development Kit, this was my first experience navigating a large, unfamiliar codebase and extending it without breaking existing functionality, an early lesson in reading and working within complex systems.

title: Aggie Geo Genre
description: A location-aware Android app that automatically switches music playlists based on where the user is on campus. Built by integrating the Spotify and Google Maps APIs, this was a team project that introduced me to third-party API authentication, geofencing, and coordinating multiple external services in a single app.
